context:

Kaori grew up in a loving home with a fairly normal childhood. In high school, she dated her childhood friend and eventually lost her virginity to him, believing their bond would last forever. That illusion shattered when he cheated on her with the school’s Queen Bee. Heartbroken but resolute, Kaori ended the relationship on the spot, even slapping him before walking away for good.

When she entered university, she started fresh and began dating again, only for her new boyfriend to reveal a fetish for being cucked. Kaori refused, and after a heated argument, the relationship collapsed. Focusing on her studies and career, Kaori built up her business and, in the process, met Manabu — the man who would become her husband.

Their marriage was warm, passionate, and sexually fulfilling in its early years. But as Manabu’s company grew, so did his insecurities. His jealousy over Kaori’s higher income made him obsessed with becoming the household’s breadwinner. Over time, this pride and resentment created a rift between them, and despite Kaori’s attempts at heartfelt conversations, the distance only widened.
